goal: Add self-documenting formalization layer interfaces to the public HarnessIQ repository surface. The delivered scope is a minimal SDK-facing interface addition: a base formalization layer abstract class plus a focused subset of typed formalization layer base classes that can later be wired into harness runtime injection.
success_criteria: 1. The repository exports a new formalization interface module from the public `harnessiq.interfaces` package.
2. The new module defines a self-documenting base formalization abstraction with documented runtime hook methods and developer-facing description helpers.
3. The new module includes a focused subset of typed formalization layer base classes derived from the conversation, without overcommitting the runtime implementation.
4. Targeted tests cover the new public exports and the core self-documenting behavior.
5. The change avoids unrelated runtime rewrites and does not disturb existing dirty worktree changes.

in_scope: Add one new interface module under `harnessiq/interfaces/` for formalization contracts.
Add focused tests for the new interface surface.
Create and maintain durable mission artifacts and implementation notes under `memory/formalization-layer-interfaces/`.
out_of_scope: Integrating formalization layers into `BaseAgent` runtime execution in this change.
Adding every possible concrete formalization implementation described in the design conversation.
Regenerating repository-wide generated docs or broad SDK refactors unrelated to the new contracts.

constraints: Do not revert or disturb unrelated dirty worktree changes.
Treat `harnessiq/` as the only authoritative runtime source tree.
Keep the repository change minimal and centered on the interfaces package plus tests.
Prefer self-documenting abstractions that can describe both runtime behavior and model-facing context without committing to a full runtime implementation yet.

allowed_without_additional_approval: Create and update mission artifacts under `memory/formalization-layer-interfaces/`.
Edit interface and test files needed for the scoped implementation.
requires_human_approval: Broad runtime integration beyond the interface layer.
Pushing branches, creating PRs, or deleting user-created issues.

human_contact.urgency: Surface only blocking ambiguities that would materially change the interface contract.
